extra.reasoning:
severe_headache appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100), IF (rash and fever and slurred_speech and syncope and severe_headache) THEN acuity := self_care. This is the highest-priority rule and it sets the LOWEST acuity. Because severe_headache is a required conjunct, toggling it flips this rule on/off.

Compare the baseline input rash=T, fever=T, slurred_speech=T, syncope=T with severe_headache=FALSE (all others false): interaction_drop does not match. The highest-priority matching rules are raise_fever (priority 3) and raise_syncope (priority 3), both yielding emergent. So acuity = emergent (the highest level).

Now set severe_headache=TRUE with everything else unchanged: all five conjuncts of interaction_drop are now true, so it matches. With priority 100 it dominates all priority-3 rules, setting acuity := self_care (the lowest level).

Thus flipping severe_headache from false to true lowers acuity from emergent to self_care, directly violating the monotonicity property that setting severe_headache true must never lower acuity. The witness_assignment given is the post-toggle (severe_headache=true) state; the corresponding pre-toggle state differs only in severe_headache=false and yields emergent.
